folio.label: 134r
folio.l: And whan this Maister , that this Magyk wrougút
Saw it was tyme , he clapt hise handes two
And fare wel , al oure reuel was a goo
And remeeued they neuäe , out of the hous
While they saugú , al this sigút merueillous
But in his studie , there as hise bookes bee
They seten stille , but no wygút , but they three
To hi~ this maistä called , his squyere
And seide hi~ thus , is redy oure soupere
Almost an houre it is , I vndertake
Sithe I ©ow bad , oure souper for to make
Whan that theise worthy men , wenten witú me
In to my studie , there as my bookes be
¢ Sire q¶ this Squyere , whan that it lyketú ©ow
It is al redy , thougú ©e wol rigút now
Go we thanne Soupe , q¶ he , for the beste
Theise amerous folk , somtyme mot han here reste
¢ At after Souper , fel they in tretee
What so~me , shulde this maistres , gerdoun be
To remeeuen alle the Rokkes , of Brytaigne
And eke from gerounde , to the moutú of Sayne
He mad it straunge , and swor so god hi~ saue
Lasse than a thou©sand pound , he wolde nat haue
Ne gladly for that somme , he wolde nat gon
Aurelius , witú blisful herte a noon
Answered thus , fy on a thousand pound
This wide world , whicú that men seye is round
I wolde it ©eue , if I were lord of it
This bargeyn is ful dreue , for we ben knyt
ñe shal be paid trewely , by my trouthe
But loketú now , for no necligence , or slouthe
ñe tarie vs heere , no lengere than to morwe
Nay q¶ this Clerk , haue heere my feitú to borwe
To bedde is gon Aurelius , whan hi~ leste
And wel ny al that nygút , he had his reste
What for his labour , and his hope of blisse
His wooful herte , of penaunce had a lisse
¢ Vp on the morwe , whan that it was day
To Britaigne toke they , the rigút way
Aurelius , and this Magicien byside
And ben descended , there they wolde a byde
And this was , as theise bookes me remembre
The colde frosty Sesoun , of Decembre
¢ Phebus wex old , and hewed like latoun
